content.problemStatement:
You are given a string SSS of length NNN, containing only the characters `a`, `b`, and `c`.

In one move, you can modify SSS as follows:


Choose three indices i,j,ki, j, ki,j,k (1≤i<j<k≤∣S∣1 \leq i \lt j \lt k \leq |S|1≤i<j<k≤∣S∣) such that Si=a,Sj=b,Sk=cS_i = \texttt{a}, S_j = \texttt{b}, S_k = \texttt{c}Si​=a,Sj​=b,Sk​=c.

That is, choose some subsequence of SSS that equals `abc`.
Then, delete either the `a` or the `c` from SSS, that is, either index iii or index kkk.

Note that this reduces the length of SSS by 111.
For example, if S=cbba‾cb‾ac‾cS = \texttt{cbb\underline{a}c\underline{b}a\underline{c}c}S=cbba​cb​ac​c, you can choose i=4,j=6,k=8i = 4, j = 6, k = 8i=4,j=6,k=8 (the underlined indices), and then delete either index 444 (obtaining S=cbbcbaccS = \texttt{cbbcbacc}S=cbbcbacc) or index 888 (obtaining S=cbbacbacS = \texttt{cbbacbac}S=cbbacbac).

Find the **minimum** number of moves that can be made on SSS, such that it's impossible to perform any further moves on the resulting string.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN — the length of SSS.
The second line of each test case contains the string SSS of length NNN.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line minimum possible number of moves that can be made on SSS, such that it's not possible to perform any further moves.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 3⋅1053\cdot 10^53⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Choose i=1,j=2,k=3i = 1, j = 2, k = 3i=1,j=2,k=3 and delete index 111. Now, S=bccS = \texttt{bcc}S=bcc and no more moves can be performed.
Test case 222: No moves can be performed for the given string, so the answer is 000.
Test case 333: One optimal solution is as follows:

Choose i=2,j=6,k=7i = 2, j = 6, k = 7i=2,j=6,k=7, and delete index 777.
Now, S=aabacbS = \texttt{aabacb}S=aabacb.
Choose i=2,j=3,k=5i = 2, j = 3, k = 5i=2,j=3,k=5, and delete index 555.
Now, S=aababS = \texttt{aabab}S=aabab, and no more moves can be performed.

It can be shown that using less than 222 moves is not possible.
